seoTitle: How to Copy a Page to Another Project in Webflow
metaDescription: Move a complete page layout between two Webflow projects using the designer's cross-project copy and paste workflow.
intro: Reusing a high-converting landing page or migrating a design system between Webflow sites is possible without rebuilding from scratch. This guide covers grouping a page inside a root wrapper, copying it across projects, and resolving style conflicts that come up during the paste. It's for freelancers and agencies managing multiple Webflow sites.

steps.heading: Group the page in a root wrapper
steps.body: Select all elements on the page and nest them inside a single root Div block so the entire layout can be copied as one unit.

steps.heading: Copy the wrapper
steps.body: Select the root wrapper and use the copy keyboard shortcut to place the full layout on your clipboard.

steps.heading: Open the destination project
steps.body: Switch to the target Webflow project, open the Pages panel, and create a new blank page to paste into.

steps.heading: Paste and resolve class conflicts
steps.body: Paste the layout onto the new page, then check the Style Manager for duplicate class names that Webflow may have auto-suffixed.

steps.heading: Reconnect fonts and interactions
steps.body: Re-link any missing custom fonts, re-trigger element-based interactions, then check responsiveness before publishing.

faq.question: Will copying a page bring over its custom fonts automatically?
faq.answer: Not always, so check your typography settings in the destination project and re-link any custom font packages that didn't transfer.

faq.question: What happens if both projects have a class with the same name?
faq.answer: Webflow automatically appends a numerical suffix to the duplicate class in the destination project to avoid overwriting existing styles.

faq.question: Do interactions carry over when copying a page?
faq.answer: Interaction triggers can be inconsistent across projects, so it's worth double-checking and re-triggering any GSAP-based animations after pasting.

faq.question: Can I copy just one section instead of a whole page?
faq.answer: Yes, the same copy and paste approach works for a single section or component, not just an entire page.
